capability_objective: The communications lead is auditing two outbound-message entries before the weekly send review. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the message named “Donor Reception Follow-Up” at “Advancement” and the message named “Exhibit Opening Invitation” at “Museum Members”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Return only a two-row Markdown table with columns Record, ID, Date, and Status, preserving the requested order.
prompt:
The communications lead needs a checked outbound-message audit before the weekly send review. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `message-audit.md` from full message records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `MESSAGES.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./messagectl` executable for every registry lookup; discover its interface exactly once from the top-level built-in help before accessing message data. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed or key, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the message named “Donor Reception Follow-Up” at “Advancement” and the message named “Exhibit Opening Invitation” at “Museum Members”. Your first message-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./messagectl` processes, with no other registry operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match containing one nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following message-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./messagectl` processes and wait for both. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take every reported record name, stable ID, date, and status for a resolved branch only from its successful full-record get. Compare the two retrieved status strings without normalizing them.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter registry data, inspect or edit the execution journal, expose unrelated records, or include other full-record fields. Do not invoke any registry operation beyond interface discovery, the two required searches, and eligible full-record gets.

`message-audit.md` must contain only one two-row Markdown table: exactly four nonblank, newline-terminated lines, with no heading, blank lines, code fence, or other prose. The first line must be `| Record | ID | Date | Status |` and the second line must be `|---|---|---|---|`. Put Donor Reception Follow-Up in the first data row and Exhibit Opening Invitation in the second. Format a resolved row exactly as `| <record name> | <stable ID> | <date> | <status> |`, substituting only values from that branch's full record and preserving spelling and punctuation. Format an unresolved row exactly as `| <requested name> | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED |`. Include no literal angle brackets. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied files unchanged and make the final assistant response exactly the table's content.
